results.key_finding: Strong negative correlation between o_orderdate<9204 and l_shipdate>9204: only 0.21 qualifying lineitems per qualifying order (vs ~2.16 independent estimate). The query guide's ~3.5M agg group estimate is 11x too high; empirical data gives ~305K groups.

join_order.probe: orders(o_orderdate<9204) via zone-map-guided scan → check o_custkey in bitset
join_order.rationale: Smallest filtered table first. Custkey bitset fits in L2 cache (187KB), enabling ultra-fast membership checks during orders scan.

join_order.build: qualifying orders → compact_hash_map[o_orderkey → (o_orderdate, o_shippriority)] (~1.46M entries, ~47MB) + bloom_filter
join_order.probe: lineitem(l_shipdate>9204) via zone-map-guided scan → bloom check → hash probe
join_order.rationale: Bloom filter (1.84MB, L3-resident) eliminates 99.04% of ship-qualifying lineitem rows before expensive hash table probe. Sequential scan of lineitem far outperforms random index lookups (lineitem_orderkey_hash would require 5.88M random accesses vs 32.4M sequential with 99% bloom rejection).

aggregation_details.group_count_basis: empirically measured from 1% sample; bounded by qualifying orders (1.46M) but much less due to low lineitems-per-qualifying-order ratio (0.21)

filter_pushdown.acceleration: zone_map on o_orderdate — skip blocks where block_min >= 9204 (~51% of 150 blocks skipped)
zone_map on l_shipdate — skip blocks where block_max <= 9204 (~46% of 600 blocks = 276 blocks skipped, 324 qualifying)

filter_pushdown.method: bloom_filter (1.84MB, L3-resident) eliminates 99.04% before compact_hash_map probe

customer_custkey_filter.rationale: c_custkey range is [1, 1500000] — dense integer domain, direct-indexed bitset fits in L2 cache (512KB), O(1) lookup with zero cache pressure

orders_qualifying_map.rationale: Pre-sized to avoid rehashing; open-addressing with Robin Hood / linear probing. NOT std::unordered_map.

orderkey_bloom_filter.rationale: 1.47M qualifying orderkeys; ~18MB target fits within L3 cache (44MB); eliminates 99.04% of ship-qualifying lineitem rows before expensive hash_map probe

aggregation.value: struct { double revenue_sum; int32_t o_orderdate; int32_t o_shippriority; }
aggregation.rationale: 304,700 groups empirically measured (not 3.5M as guide estimated). Thread-local maps with sequential merge; 64 × 20MB = 1.28GB total thread-local memory, well within 376GB RAM.

data_structures.topk: min_heap(size=10) keyed on (revenue DESC, o_orderdate ASC)

phases.rationale: 1.5M rows × 1 byte = 1.5MB — single thread completes in <1ms

phases.details: Thread-local qualifying order lists; atomic morsel counter; zone-map-guided block skip at thread-granularity. Merge into shared hash_map after scan.
phases.synchronization: lock_free morsel fetch (atomic counter) + sequential merge phase

phases.details: Each thread maintains thread-local aggregation hash_map. Zone-map block assignment at thread level. Bloom filter checked per row before hash probe.
phases.synchronization: lock_free morsel fetch (atomic counter) + sequential merge of 64 thread-local agg maps

phases.details: Merge 64 thread-local maps (~304,700 groups each) into single map; then extract top-10 via min-heap

parallelism.partition_count: N/A (thread-local maps, not partitioned by key)

index_usage.purpose: Skip blocks where block_min >= 9204 (orders sorted by o_orderdate ascending). ~51% of 150 blocks skipped. Reduces orders scan from 15M to ~7.3M rows.
index_usage.layout: uint32_t num_blocks=150; then [int32_t min, int32_t max] × 150

index_usage.purpose: Skip blocks where block_max <= 9204 (lineitem sorted by l_shipdate ascending). ~46% of 600 blocks skipped. Reduces lineitem scan from 60M to ~32.4M rows.
index_usage.layout: uint32_t num_blocks=600; then [int32_t min, int32_t max] × 600

index_usage.purpose: SKIPPED: Would require 300K custkey probes → ~4.5M random accesses into positions array and o_orderdate column. The zone-map-guided sequential scan with L2-resident custkey bitset is faster on 64-core machine: sequential memory access pattern parallelizes to full DRAM bandwidth, whereas random index lookups produce cache misses across the 60MB positions array (exceeds L3).

SKIPPED: Would require 1.46M order probes → ~5.88M random accesses into lineitem_shipdate column (240MB, far exceeds 44MB L3 → cold cache misses). Sequential zone-map scan + bloom filter is more efficient: 32.4M sequential reads with 99.04% bloom rejection leaves only 311K hash probes. This is 19x fewer hash table probes than the index-driven approach.

scan_details.customer: single_pass: scan c_mktsegment.bin (1.5M × 1B = 1.5MB), collect qualifying c_custkey into bitset
scan_details.orders: zone_map_guided_selective: load orderdate zone_map first, skip blocks where block_min>=9204; fused scan of o_orderdate + o_custkey for predicate evaluation; late-load o_orderkey + o_shippriority only for qualifying rows
scan_details.lineitem: zone_map_guided_selective: load shipdate zone_map first, skip blocks where block_max<=9204; fused scan of l_shipdate + l_orderkey for predicate evaluation; bloom filter on l_orderkey before hash probe; late-load l_extendedprice + l_discount only for rows passing both filters

column_load_order: indexes/orders_orderdate_zonemap.bin (zone maps first — tiny, enables selective loading)
orders/o_orderkey.bin (payload — late, only for qualifying rows)
orders/o_shippriority.bin (payload — late, only for qualifying rows)
lineitem/l_orderkey.bin (filter column — join predicate, bloom check before hash probe)
lineitem/l_extendedprice.bin (payload — late, only for ~309K qualifying rows)
lineitem/l_discount.bin (payload — late, only for ~309K qualifying rows)
io_strategy.mmap_strategy: mmap + madvise(MADV_WILLNEED) on qualifying blocks only (zone-map guided)

late_materialization.orders: Load o_orderkey and o_shippriority only for rows passing o_orderdate<9204 AND o_custkey-in-bitset
late_materialization.lineitem: Load l_extendedprice and l_discount only for rows passing l_shipdate>9204 AND bloom AND l_orderkey-in-orders-hash

phase_breakdown.orders_zone_map_scan: ~5ms (zone-map skips 51% of blocks; 7.3M rows × 2 cols × 4B = 58MB sequential; parallelized 64 threads)
phase_breakdown.lineitem_zone_map_scan_with_bloom: ~30ms (zone-map skips 46% of blocks; 32.4M rows × 2 cols × 4B = 260MB sequential; 99% bloom rejection limits hash probes to ~311K)
phase_breakdown.total_estimated: ~61ms warm cache; ~150-200ms cold-start HDD (zone-map reduces I/O 2x)

estimated_cost.bottleneck: Lineitem zone-map scan (260MB sequential, 64-thread parallel) + aggregation merge
